q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is logging.info, which writes to stdout/stderr and is not dangerous in a test environment.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Violation is the act of logging the password in cleartext at line 26; function returns nothing (kind: none) and no post-return state reveals the violation.

mock_guidance.target: Mock the logging.info call invoked at line 26 within the http_brute function.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the arguments passed to logging.info, specifically checking whether the formatted string contains the password variable in cleartext.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Allow the mock to consume the call without writing to any real output stream, so execution continues through the loop.
notes: The violation occurs inside a loop over passwords; the mock should capture each invocation to confirm that every password is logged in cleartext.
Optionally mock the HTTP request (requests.get) to avoid real network calls while still testing the logging behavior.
